title: Logo delivery misses the agreed brief
question: Should the payee be paid under the linked terms, given the client claims the delivered logo uses the wrong color system and ships without vector files?

evidence.label: Signed project brief listing the required deliverables and color spec
Final files the designer handed over (one PNG, one JPG, no vector source)
Client message rejecting the delivery, with a side-by-side of the brief colors and the supplied art
Designer's reply offering to convert to vector but disputing the color claim

terms: Avery Quinn (designer) agreed to deliver a final logo to Northbrook Bakery for 800 USD, held in escrow. The brief, signed on 2026-04-02, requires the logo built in the CMYK values listed there (warm brown 0/35/60/40 and cream 0/4/18/2), plus editable vector source files in SVG and AI format alongside the raster exports. Payment releases only when both the color spec and all four file formats are met.
